officials as described in Government Code Section 86111. 
Your 
letter of March 22, 1988 accurately summarizes my telephone 
advice. 
We consider this advice to be informal assistance 
pursuant to 2 Cal. Code of Regs. section 18329(c) because you 
have not informed us of the identity of the person on whose 
behalf you are seeking advice. 
(Regulation 18329(c) (3) (C).)l1 
If you have any questions regarding this letter, please 
contact me a (916) 322-5901.

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Kirk Allen Pessner 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
March 24, 1988 
650 California street, suite 2650 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Re: 
88-120 
Dear Mr. Pessner: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on March 23, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in 
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
• 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 
18329) . ) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
(vv---"' 
) 1, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: How should activity expenses be reported to agency officials under Government Code Section 86111, and what constitutes an 'agency' for purposes of disclosure requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed telephone advice that Government Code Section 86111 requires disclosure of activity expenses to agency officials, where 'agency' is defined as any state agency that the reporting person's firm, lobbyist employer, or client is attempting to influence.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of multiple related pieces: incoming request letter (March 22, 1988), acknowledgment letter (March 24, 1988), and FPPC response letter (April 21, 1988). Primary response is informal advice letter dated April 21, 1988. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; information extracted from body text. Document lacks formal structure typical of formal advice letters. Identity of person seeking advice was not disclosed, resulting in classification as informal assistance rather than formal written advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should activity expenses be reported to agency officials under Government Code Section 86111, and what constitutes an 'agency' for purposes of disclosure requirements?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC confirmed telephone advice that Government Code Section 86111 requires disclosure of activity expenses to agency officials, where 'agency' is defined as any state agency that the reporting person's firm, lobbyist employer, or client is attempting to influence.

embedding.summary: This is an informal advice letter from the FPPC confirming telephone advice provided to a law firm regarding the reporting of activity expenses to agency officials under Government Code Section 86111. The advice letter clarifies the definition of 'agency' for disclosure purposes.
